response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software engineer with strong web, mobile, and tooling credentials, but his profile has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep hardware expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his resume. His most relevant experience is low-latency software work in D at Sociomantic and recent AI agent tooling at re:cinq, neither of which approaches the hardware-level depth this role demands.
strengths: 14+ years of professional software experience satisfies the BS + 8yr education/experience threshold
Demonstrated performance-oriented systems work (low-latency, async I/O, high-throughput microservices at Sociomantic)
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and agentic workflows at re:cinq shows awareness of the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero SoC/IP modeling, SIMD/MIMD architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in deep learning, neural network training/inference optimization, or GPU architecture and workload analysis
Entire career is web, backend, and tooling software development with no cloud infrastructure at the hardware optimization layer

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU experience
